label: Kyiv
sub: Capital city; survived 2022 siege; sustained missile/drone strikes throughout war

label: Kharkiv
sub: Ukraine's 2nd city; survived 2022 capture attempt; near active frontline; under daily Russian shelling

label: Mariupol
sub: Besieged and occupied May 2022; Azovstal defenders' last stand; city largely destroyed

label: Bakhmut
sub: Site of war's longest, bloodiest battle (Aug 2022–May 2023); captured by Wagner Group

label: Avdiivka
sub: Ukrainian stronghold since 2014; fell to Russia Feb 18, 2024 — Russia's biggest gain of 2024

label: Kherson
sub: Regional capital; liberated Nov 11, 2022; still shelled from Russian positions across Dnipro

label: Zaporizhzhia
sub: Frontline regional center; under regular Russian missile and drone attacks

label: Zaporizhzhia NPP
sub: Europe's largest nuclear plant; Russian-occupied since Mar 4, 2022; 6 reactors shut down; IAEA mission ongoing

label: Odesa
sub: Key Black Sea port city; major Russian strike target; critical for Ukrainian grain exports; protected by air defense

label: Dnipro
sub: Major industrial city; key logistics hub; sustained major missile strikes including Jan 2023 apartment building attack

label: Kramatorsk
sub: Administrative center of Ukrainian-held Donetsk Oblast; regular Russian strike target; railroad hub

label: Izyum
sub: Key Russian logistics hub liberated Sep 2022 counteroffensive; mass graves found post-liberation

label: Melitopol
sub: Russian-controlled city; key hub on land corridor to Crimea; primary target of failed 2023 counteroffensive

label: Bucha
sub: Site of documented Russian war crimes; hundreds of civilian bodies found after Russian withdrawal Apr 2022

label: Soledar
sub: Salt-mining town captured by Wagner Jan 2023; precursor to Bakhmut fall

label: Kakhovka Dam
sub: Destroyed Jun 6, 2023; caused catastrophic flooding; 1M+ lost water access; Russia accused of sabotage

label: Kerch Bridge
sub: Partially destroyed by Ukraine Oct 8, 2022 and Oct 2023; key supply route to occupied Crimea

label: Sevastopol
sub: Russian Black Sea Fleet HQ in occupied Crimea; targeted by Ukrainian drone and missile strikes throughout war

label: Tokmak
sub: Key Russian logistics hub in occupied Zaporizhzhia; primary HIMARS strike target; goal of 2023 Ukrainian counteroffensive

label: Pokrovsk
sub: Critical Ukrainian logistics hub in Donetsk; under intense Russian pressure since 2024; key to Ukrainian supply lines

label: Lyman
sub: Railroad hub liberated by Ukraine in Sep 2022 Kharkiv counteroffensive; key rail hub for Donbas

label: Lviv
sub: Key western logistics hub for NATO weapons deliveries; Polish border crossing point; major refugee transit city

label: Orikhiv
sub: Frontline town in Zaporizhzhia Oblast; focal point of 2023 Ukrainian counteroffensive breakthrough attempts

label: Dnipro River Line
sub: Active front along Dnipro River east of Kherson; Ukraine holds west bank; Russia east bank since Nov 2022

label: Chasiv Yar
sub: Key elevated position near Bakhmut; under intense Russian pressure since 2024; controls approaches to Kramatorsk

label: Mykolaiv
sub: Southern city; defended Ukrainian flank protecting Odesa; sustained Russian strikes throughout 2022

label: Sumy
sub: Border city; under frequent Russian cross-border shelling; Ukrainian forces used Sumy as staging for Kursk incursion

label: Donetsk City
sub: Russian-controlled administrative center of occupied Donetsk Oblast; occupied since 2014; front lines 10–15 km from city center

label: Kharkiv-Belgorod Front
sub: Active Russian cross-border pressure zone; Russia opened offensive from Belgorod into Kharkiv Oblast May 2024

label: Polish Border Crossing
sub: Primary NATO weapons and aid entry point into Ukraine; Medyka/Shehyni crossings handle bulk of military deliveries

label: Patriot Air Defense
sub: Patriot PAC-3 batteries positioned to defend Kyiv; successfully intercepted Kinzhal hypersonic missiles in May 2023

label: Snake Island
sub: Small island in Black Sea; captured by Russia Feb 2022; retaken by Ukraine Aug 7, 2022; site of famous Ukrainian defiance

label: Vuhledar
sub: Strategic mining town; Ukraine inflicted major Russian armored losses in early 2023; fell to Russia Oct 2024

label: Krasnohorivka
sub: Frontline town near Donetsk; contested throughout 2023–2024; Russia captured portions in 2024

label: Luhansk City
sub: Administrative center of Russian-occupied Luhansk Oblast; held by Russia since 2014

label: Kherson Oblast Front
sub: Southern front along Dnipro River; Ukraine holds Kherson city; Russia holds east bank including Oleshky

label: Kharkiv Aid Hub
sub: UNHCR and international NGO coordination hub for northeast Ukraine; critical humanitarian logistics center

label: Crimea Peninsula
sub: Russian-occupied since 2014; formally annexed; HQ of Black Sea Fleet; targeted by Ukrainian long-range strikes

label: Lipetsk Air Base (Russia)
sub: Russian air base targeted by Ukrainian long-range drones in 2024; housed Su-34 aircraft used for strikes

label: Sudzha (Russia – Kursk)
sub: Largest town captured by Ukraine in Aug 2024 Kursk incursion; recaptured by Russia Mar 2025

label: Izmail Port
sub: Danube river port; Russia attacked grain infrastructure here after withdrawing from Black Sea grain deal Jul 2023

label: Sloviansk
sub: Northern anchor of Ukraine's 'Fortress Belt'; under Russian pressure since spring 2026 offensive; mandatory evacuations ordered Mar 2026

label: Kostiantynivka
sub: Southern anchor of 'Fortress Belt'; heavily contested in 2026 spring offensive; key Ukrainian defensive position in Donetsk Oblast

label: Poltava
sub: Regional center; energy infrastructure struck in Russia's March 24, 2026 mass missile attack causing regional power outages

label: Ilsky Refinery (Krasnodar)
sub: Russian oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai struck by Ukrainian drones March 27, 2026; second consecutive night of strikes on Russian energy in region

label: Odesa Maternity Hospital Strike
sub: Russia struck Odesa with 60+ drones on March 28, 2026, hitting a maternity hospital roof, 3 educational institutions, and apartment buildings; 2 killed, 11 injured; 81 evacuated

label: Kovsharovka (Captured)
sub: Captured by Russian 1st Guards Tank Army on March 29, 2026 — largest urbanized settlement (pop. ~17,960) seized in Kharkiv Oblast spring offensive; Russian forces pressing toward Kupyansk-Uzlovoi

label: Ozernoye Airbase (Zhytomyr)
sub: Ukrainian military airbase struck by at least 7 Russian Geran-2 kamikaze UAVs on March 29, 2026; base serves combined combat aviation and personnel training

label: Kryvyi Rih Industrial Strike
sub: Russian drone struck an industrial site in Kryvyi Rih on March 29, 2026, killing 1 man and igniting fires; part of 442-drone overnight barrage

label: Taganrog (Russia — Struck)
sub: Ukrainian drone strike killed 1 and wounded several in Taganrog, Rostov Oblast on March 31, 2026; dozens of residential buildings damaged; part of Ukraine's deep-strike campaign

label: Nikopol Drone Strike
sub: Russian drone strike on April 1, 2026 injured 11 people including a child in Nikopol, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast; infrastructure damage reported

label: Podoly (Russian Claim)
sub: Russian MoD claimed seizure of Podoly village near Kupyansk, Kharkiv Oblast on April 1, 2026; Ukrainian GS did not confirm; Russian forces pressing toward Kupyansk-Uzlovoi

label: Chuhuiv Strike (Apr 2)
sub: Russian drone struck a residential building in Chuhuiv, Kharkiv Oblast on April 2, 2026, igniting fires on two floors and injuring 3 women; part of continued Russian strikes on Kharkiv Oblast civilian infrastructure

label: Bryansk Missile Factory (UA Strike)
sub: Ukraine struck a manufacturing components facility for 'Strela' cruise missiles in Bryansk region on April 2, 2026; part of the ongoing deep-strike campaign against Russian defense-industrial infrastructure

label: Kirovske Airfield (UA Strike, Apr 3)
sub: Ukrainian military intelligence struck Kirovske military airfield in occupied Crimea overnight April 2–3, 2026; part of sustained campaign to degrade Russian air assets in the peninsula

label: Kharkiv Ballistic Strike (Apr 3)
sub: Russia fired 4 ballistic missiles at Kharkiv on April 3, 2026, striking residential buildings and igniting a fire in the Osnovianskyi district; 1 woman injured; Kharkiv has endured 20+ strikes in early April alone

label: Novorossiysk Energy Hub (UA Strike)
sub: Ukraine struck Russian Black Sea energy hub at Novorossiysk on April 6, 2026, expanding its deep-strike campaign against Russian energy infrastructure beyond oil refineries to Black Sea export terminals

label: Odesa Port Strike (Apr 8)
sub: Russian drone struck critical infrastructure at Odesa port on April 8, 2026, continuing Moscow's campaign to disrupt Ukraine's primary Black Sea maritime export hub

label: Belarus Border Road Construction (Apr 18)
sub: New road construction near the Belarus-Ukraine border raises fears of Belarusian re-entry into the conflict, mirroring logistics staging seen before the 2022 invasion.

label: Tuapse Oil Refinery (Struck)
sub: Ukraine struck Russia's Tuapse oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai twice in one week — April 16 ('volcano' fires) and April 20 (confirmed second strike, 1 killed). Refinery processes ~12 million tons of crude annually; part of Ukraine's sustained campaign against Russian energy export infrastructure.

label: Nizhyn (Chernihiv Oblast)
sub: Russian drone and rocket strikes on April 25, 2026 killed 2 people and injured 2 others. 27 attacks reported in the Nizhyn area in the preceding day. Part of Russia's mass 666-UAV/missile attack, one of the largest of the year.

label: Huliaipole (Zaporizhzhia)
sub: Active frontline sector in Zaporizhzhia Oblast under intensifying Russian pressure in April 2026. Russian forces advancing west of Gulyaypole; 18 combat engagements on April 26. Civilian minibus struck by guided aerial bomb, killing 1 and injuring 4.

label: Chornomorsk Port (Oil Spill)
sub: Russian drone strike on April 27, 2026 hit port infrastructure including a storage tank, causing a ~6,000-ton sunflower oil spill into the Black Sea — a major environmental disaster. Chornomorsk is a key Ukrainian grain and commodity export hub 30 km SW of Odesa.
